item.title: Beating the Market in Zimbabwe: Why Marketing and Sales Capabilities Are the New Competitive Edge
item.description: IN Zimbabwe’s volatile and often unforgiving business environment, consistent growth is difficult. Sustained outperformance is even rarer. Yet a growing body of evidence suggests that the difference between companies that merely survive and those that lead the market lies not in capital investment or scale alone, but in the strength of their marketing and sales […]The post Beating the Market in Zimbabwe: Why Marketing and Sales Capabilities Are the New Competitive Edge appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Marketing Return on Investment in Zimbabwe: From Spend to Strategy in a Constrained Economy
item.description: IN Zimbabwe’s fragile and highly competitive economic environment, marketing is no longer judged by visibility alone. It is increasingly evaluated by one question: what does it deliver? By Our Insights Team With constrained budgets, currency volatility, and intensifying competition across sectors, businesses are under pressure to ensure that every dollar spent on marketing translates into […]The post Marketing Return on Investment in Zimbabwe: From Spend to Strategy in a Constrained Economy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Consumer & Shopper Insights in Zimbabwe: Turning Behaviour into Business Growth
item.description: IN Zimbabwe’s volatile and highly adaptive economy, understanding the consumer is no longer a marketing luxury—it is a strategic necessity. Businesses that succeed are not always those with the largest capital base, but those that best interpret how customers think, spend, and adapt under pressure. By Our Insights Team From informal traders in Mbare to […]The post Consumer & Shopper Insights in Zimbabwe: Turning Behaviour into Business Growth appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Branding in Zimbabwe: Navigating Complexity in a Digital, Volatile Market
item.description: Branding has taken on a more strategic and consequential role in Zimbabwe’s business landscape, evolving far beyond visual identity and advertising campaigns. In an economy shaped by currency instability, shifting consumer behaviour, and the rise of digital platforms, brands are now central to how companies secure trust, defend margins, and sustain growth. While global forces […]The post Branding in Zimbabwe: Navigating Complexity in a Digital, Volatile Market appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Real-time digital traffic surveillance systems
item.description: GOVERNMENT has concluded the development of an automated smart traffic management system capable of detecting road violations in real time, and is ready to launch it on a full scale across the country. This marks a major shift towards technology-driven law enforcement aimed at improving road safety and reducing human interference. The system, developed by […]The post Real-time digital traffic surveillance systems appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe Projects Cereal Surplus as Soyabean Output Surges
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe is projecting a significant cereal surplus of up to 964,945 metric tonnes by March 2027, following a strong recovery in agricultural output during the 2025/2026 summer season, according to a Cabinet report presented this week. The update, delivered by the Ministry of Agriculture to Cabinet on 21 April, estimates total cereal production […]The post Zimbabwe Projects Cereal Surplus as Soyabean Output Surges appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Reversing Informalisation in Zimbabwe: A Long-Horizon Structural Reform Agenda in a Fragmenting Global Economy
item.description: Zimbabwe’s deepening informalisation is no longer a peripheral labour-market adjustment; it is a structural macroeconomic condition shaped by currency instability, fiscal design, institutional credibility, and the reconfiguration of global capital flows. A recent assessment by the World Bank situates the country’s trajectory within a broader pattern observed across emerging and frontier economies: once informality becomes […]The post Reversing Informalisation in Zimbabwe: A Long-Horizon Structural Reform Agenda in a Fragmenting Global Economy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: From Data Scarcity to Data Sovereignty: How Zimbabwean Business Can Leapfrog into the AI Age
item.description: IN boardrooms across Harare, Bulawayo, and Victoria Falls, a quiet but consequential shift is underway. Decisions that were once shaped by instinct, experience, and fragmented reports are increasingly being driven by data—structured, unstructured, real-time, and predictive. What was once unknowable is now discoverable within minutes. The question is no longer whether Zimbabwean businesses should embrace […]The post From Data Scarcity to Data Sovereignty: How Zimbabwean Business Can Leapfrog into the AI Age appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe
item.description: Zimbabwe stands at an unusual intersection of necessity and opportunity. On one hand, the pressures are unmistakable—energy insecurity, climate variability, industrial stagnation, and constrained capital. On the other, the country possesses the raw ingredients of a green transition: abundant sunlight, agricultural depth, mineral wealth, and a youthful population increasingly attuned to technology and sustainability. The […]The post Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: AI as the New Industrial Engine: How Zimbabwean Businesses Can Build Faster, Smarter, and Globally Competitive Ventures
item.description: Artificial intelligence is no longer a distant technological promise—it is rapidly becoming the foundation upon which modern businesses are built. Across the world, companies are using AI to compress development timelines, reduce operating costs, and scale at unprecedented speed. For Zimbabwe, where businesses operate within tight financial constraints and structural inefficiencies, AI presents more than […]The post AI as the New Industrial Engine: How Zimbabwean Businesses Can Build Faster, Smarter, and Globally Competitive Ventures appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Land Incentives Framework Sparks Political Storm as Critics Accuse Kudakwashe Tagwirei of Overreach
item.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe’s newly announced land purchase incentives framework—positioned by the government as a tool to broaden access to land and support agricultural productivity—has triggered a wave of backlash from opposition figures, former ruling party insiders, and governance watchdogs, who allege abuse of office, lack of transparency, and creeping state capture. The policy, unveiled on Friday […]The post Land Incentives Framework Sparks Political Storm as Critics Accuse Kudakwashe Tagwirei of Overreach appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Chatunga Mugabe Shooting Case: Victim Allegedly Paid R250,000 With Further R150,000 Promised
item.description: JOHANNESBURG — New details have emerged in the high-profile shooting case involving Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, with a South African court hearing that the victim was allegedly paid R250,000 in cash and promised an additional R150,000 following the incident. Proceedings at the Alexandra Magistrate’s Court on Friday revealed that 23-year-old Sipho Mahlangu, who was shot twice […]The post Chatunga Mugabe Shooting Case: Victim Allegedly Paid R250,000 With Further R150,000 Promised appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

item.title: China’s DeepSeek rolls out a long-anticipated update of its AI model
item.description: HONG KONG — DeepSeek, the Chinese artificial intelligence startup that shook world markets last year, launched preview versions of its latest major update Friday as the AI rivalry between China and the U.S. heats up. DeepSeek’s V4 has been keenly anticipated by users keen to test how it compares to U.S. competitors like OpenAI’s ChatGPT, […]The post China’s DeepSeek rolls out a long-anticipated update of its AI model appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

item.title: Bishops warn Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 threatens Zimbabwe’s democracy
item.description: Zimbabwe’s Catholic bishops have raised concerns over the proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3, saying it may have far-reaching implications on the country’s democratic processes. In a statement, the Zimbabwe Catholic Bishops Conference (ZCBC) said the Bill, which was
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.
channel.item: Is ZANU-PF so useless that it can’t continue development without Mnangagwa? So is Zimbabwe doomed after 2030? https://www.mutapatimes.com/articles/2026-03-30-is-zanu-pf-so-useless-that-it-cant-continue-development-with.html Source: Is ZANU-PF so useless that it can’t continue development without Mnangagwa? So is Zimbabwe doomed after 2030? When praising a leader actually signals a weakness. Tendai Ruben Mbofana These are the questions that naturally arise when one examines the logic behind the Const Sat, 25 Apr 2026 09:47:10 +0000 https://www.mutapatimes.com/articles/2026-03-30-is-zanu-pf-so-useless-that-it-cant-continue-development-with.html Politics Zimbabwe Situation <media:content url="summary: "Source: Is ZANU-PF so useless that it can’t continue development without Mnangagwa? So is Zimbabwe doomed after 2030? When praising a leader actually signals a weakness. channel.title: The Mutapa Times

item.title: Zimbabwe considers social media ban for under-18s
item.description: The Zimbabwean government says it is drafting new legislation that could restrict children under the age of 18 from accessing social media platforms as part of efforts to improve online safety.The pro...
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News
